title: Itchy Rash - Atopic Dermatitis
chief_complaint: Intensely itchy rash on flexural areas (elbows and behind knees) for 6 months. Worse at night. Mother has history of asthma and hay fever.

physical_exam.skin: Erythematous, scaly patches with excoriations on antecubital and popliteal fossae, lichenification from chronic scratching, xerosis

hidden_data.skin_prick: Positive for dust mites, pollen, and cat dander

key_learning_points: Associated with asthma and allergic rhinitis (atopic triad)
Topical corticosteroids for flares, calcineurin inhibitors for maintenance

patient_responses.itching: He scratches all night, can't sleep
patient_responses.family: I have asthma and hay fever
patient_responses.triggers: It gets worse in winter and with wool clothing

hints.history: Personal or family history of atopy

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Atopic dermatitis with classic flexural involvement.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is atopic dermatitis. The flexural rash + family history + pruritus are classic.
feedback.attending_note: Atopic dermatitis: Emollients liberally (at least twice daily), topical hydrocortisone for face, triamcinolone for body. Avoid triggers (wool, harsh soaps). Short lukewarm baths. Antihistamines for pruritus at night. Most children outgrow by adolescence.
